    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e "http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"

}

  echo "    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
}

cmd_down() {
  echo "Stopping QLever..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml down
  echo "Stopping SHMARQL..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml down
}

cmd_status() {
  echo "=== QLever ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml ps
  echo ""
  echo "=== SHMARQL ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml ps
}

cmd_logs() {
  local service="${1:-}"
  if [ "$service" = "qlever" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml logs -f
  elif [ "$service" = "shmarql" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml logs -f
  else
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_mcp_add() {
  echo "Registering MCP server with Claude Code..."
  claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \
    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e "http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo "Done. Test with: claude mcp list"
}

case "${1:-}" in
  up)       cmd_up ;;
  down)     cmd_down ;;
  status)   cmd_status ;;
  logs)     cmd_logs "${2:-}" ;;
  mcp-add)  cmd_mcp_add ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh <command>"
    echo "  up        Start QLever + SHMARQL (builds index on first run)"
    echo "  down      Stop both services"
    echo "  status    Show container status"
    echo "  logs      ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    echo "  mcp-add   Register QLever as Claude Code MCP server"
    echo ""
    echo "Ports (override via env vars):"
    echo "  QLEVER_PORT=$QLEVER_PORT  (QLever SPARQL)"
    echo "  SHMARQL_PORT=$SHMARQL_PORT  (SHMARQL UI + SPARQL)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# QLever triplestore for goethe-faust DDB EDM data.
#
# First run builds the index from the NT file (a few minutes for 8.6M triples).
# Subsequent runs start in seconds — index is persisted in ./data/qlever-index/.
#
# To re-run on another server: copy this directory (including output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt),
# then run: docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml up -d --wait
#
# QLever SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:7030
# MCP server (Claude Code):
#   claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \
#     ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e http://localhost:7030

services:
  qlever-goethe-faust:
    image: docker.io/adfreiburg/qlever:latest
    ports:
      - "${QLEVER_PORT:-7030}:7019"
    volumes:
      - ./output:/input:ro
      - ./data/qlever-index:/data
    working_dir: /data
    entrypoint: ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
    command:
      - |
        set -e

        if [ -f /data/index.ready ]; then
          echo "Index already built, starting server..."
          exec /qlever/qlever-server -i /data/goethe-faust -p 7019 \
            -m $${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB} --default-query-timeout 60s
        fi

        echo "=== Goethe-Faust Setup ==="
        echo "Step 1/2: Building QLever index from NT file (8.6M triples)..."

        cat > /data/settings.json << 'SETTINGS'
        {
          "num-triples-per-batch": 500000,
          "parser-batch-size": 50000,
          "ascii-prefixes-only": false,
          "languages-internal": ["de", "en", ""],
          "prefixes-external": [
            "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/",
            "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/organization/",
            "urn:edm:Aggregation:",
            "urn:edm:ProvidedCHO:",
            "http://www.deutschefotothek.de/documents/obj/",
            "http://fotothek.slub-dresden.de/",
            "http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/",
            "http://www.w3.org/"
          ]
        }
        SETTINGS

        /qlever/qlever-index -i /data/goethe-faust \
          -f /input/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt \
          -F nt \
          -s /data/settings.json 2>&1

        touch /data/index.ready
        echo "Step 2/2: Index built."

        echo "=== Goethe-Faust QLever ready ==="
        exec /qlever/qlever-server -i /data/goethe-faust -p 7019 \
          -m $${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB} --default-query-timeout 60s
    environment:
      - QLEVER_MEMORY=${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB}
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-sf", "http://localhost:7019/?cmd=stats"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 120
      start_period: 300s

# SHMARQL (pyoxigraph-backed) for goethe-faust DDB EDM data.

# First run loads the NT file into a pyoxigraph store (~1 min for 1.3 GB).

# SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:8030/sparql
# UI:              http://localhost:8030/

services:

    image: ghcr.io/epoz/shmarql:latest
    ports:
      - "${SHMARQL_PORT:-8030}:8000"
    volumes:
      - ./output:/data:ro
      - ./data/shmarql-store:/store
    environment:
      - DATA_LOAD_PATHS=/data/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt
      - STORE_PATH=/store/goethe-faust.db

file.content:
# QLever triplestore for goethe-faust DDB EDM data.
#
# First run builds the index from the NT file (a few minutes for 8.6M triples).
# Subsequent runs start in seconds — index persisted in ./data/qlever-index/.
#
# To re-run on another server: copy this directory (including output/*.nt),
# then: docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml up -d --wait
#
# QLever SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:7030
# MCP server (Claude Code):
#   claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \
#     ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e http://localhost:7030

services:
  qlever-goethe-faust:
    image: docker.io/adfreiburg/qlever:latest
    ports:
      - "${QLEVER_PORT:-7030}:7019"
    volumes:
      - ./output:/input:ro
      - ./data/qlever-index:/data
    working_dir: /data
    entrypoint: ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
    command:
      - |
        set -e

        if [ -f /data/index.ready ]; then
          echo "Index already built, starting server..."
          exec /qlever/qlever-server -i /data/goethe-faust -p 7019 \
            -m $${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB} --default-query-timeout 60s
        fi

        echo "=== Goethe-Faust Setup ==="
        echo "Step 1/2: Building QLever index from NT file (8.6M triples)..."

        cat > /data/settings.json << 'SETTINGS'
        {
          "num-triples-per-batch": 500000,
          "parser-batch-size": 50000,
          "ascii-prefixes-only": false,
          "languages-internal": ["de", "en", ""],
          "prefixes-external": [
            "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/",
            "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/organization/",
            "urn:edm:Aggregation:",
            "urn:edm:ProvidedCHO:",
            "http://www.deutschefotothek.de/documents/obj/",
            "http://fotothek.slub-dresden.de/",
            "http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/",
            "http://www.w3.org/"
          ]
        }
        SETTINGS

        /qlever/qlever-index -i /data/goethe-faust \
          -f /input/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt \
          -F nt \
          -s /data/settings.json 2>&1

        touch /data/index.ready
        echo "Step 2/2: Index built."

        echo "=== Goethe-Faust QLever ready ==="
        exec /qlever/qlever-server -i /data/goethe-faust -p 7019 \
          -m $${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB} --default-query-timeout 60s
    environment:
      - QLEVER_MEMORY=${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB}
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-sf", "http://localhost:7019/?cmd=stats"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 120
      start_period: 300s

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Purpose:    Start/stop QLever and SHMARQL for the goethe-faust DDB EDM
#             dataset.
# Usage:      ./setup.sh <command>
#             Commands: up | down | status | logs | mcp-add
# Inputs:     output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt (1.3 GB, 8.6M triples)
# Outputs:    data/qlever-index/  — QLever binary index (persisted)
#             data/shmarql-store/ — pyoxigraph store (persisted)
# Dependencies: docker, docker compose
# Assumptions: Run from the goethe-faust/ directory.
#              On a new server, copy the full directory (including output/*.nt)
#              then run this script.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

NT_FILE="output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt"
QLEVER_PORT="${QLEVER_PORT:-7030}"
SHMARQL_PORT="${SHMARQL_PORT:-8030}"

check_prereqs() {
  if ! command -v docker &>/dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: docker not found. Install Docker Desktop or Docker Engine." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if ! docker compose version &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: 'docker compose' plugin not found." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if [ ! -f "$NT_FILE" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $NT_FILE not found." >&2
    echo "  Copy it from the source server or re-run the pipeline." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_up() {
  check_prereqs
  mkdir -p data/qlever-index data/shmarql-store

  echo "=== Starting QLever (port $QLEVER_PORT) ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml up -d --wait \
    --wait-timeout 600
  echo "  SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo ""

  echo "=== Starting SHMARQL (port $SHMARQL_PORT) ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml up -d
  echo "  SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:$SHMARQL_PORT/sparql"
  echo "  UI:              http://localhost:$SHMARQL_PORT/"
  echo ""

  echo "=== MCP server (Claude Code) ==="
  echo "  Run once to register:"
  echo "  claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \\"
  echo "    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest \\"
  echo "    -e http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
}

cmd_down() {
  echo "Stopping QLever..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml down
  echo "Stopping SHMARQL..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml down
}

cmd_status() {
  echo "=== QLever ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml ps
  echo ""
  echo "=== SHMARQL ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml ps
}

cmd_logs() {
  local service="${1:-}"
  if [ "$service" = "qlever" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml logs -f
  elif [ "$service" = "shmarql" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml logs -f
  else
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_mcp_add() {
  echo "Registering MCP server with Claude Code..."
  claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \
    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e "http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo "Done. Test with: claude mcp list"
}

case "${1:-}" in
  up)       cmd_up ;;
  down)     cmd_down ;;
  status)   cmd_status ;;
  logs)     cmd_logs "${2:-}" ;;
  mcp-add)  cmd_mcp_add ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh <command>"
    echo "  up        Start QLever + SHMARQL (builds index on first run)"
    echo "  down      Stop both services"
    echo "  status    Show container status"
    echo "  logs      ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    echo "  mcp-add   Register QLever as Claude Code MCP server"
    echo ""
    echo "Ports (override via env vars):"
    echo "  QLEVER_PORT=$QLEVER_PORT  (QLever SPARQL)"
    echo "  SHMARQL_PORT=$SHMARQL_PORT  (SHMARQL UI + SPARQL)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Purpose:    Start/stop QLever and SHMARQL for the goethe-faust DDB EDM
#             dataset.
# Usage:      ./setup.sh <command>
#             Commands: up | down | status | logs | mcp-add
# Inputs:     output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt (1.3 GB, 8.6M triples)
# Outputs:    data/qlever-index/  — QLever binary index (persisted)
#             data/shmarql-store/ — pyoxigraph store (persisted)
# Dependencies: docker, docker compose
# Assumptions: Run from the goethe-faust/ directory.
#              On a new server, copy the full directory (including output/*.nt)
#              then run this script.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

NT_FILE="output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt"
QLEVER_PORT="${QLEVER_PORT:-7030}"
YASGUI_PORT="${YASGUI_PORT:-7031}"
SHMARQL_PORT="${SHMARQL_PORT:-8030}"

check_prereqs() {
  if ! command -v docker &>/dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: docker not found. Install Docker Desktop or Docker Engine." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if ! docker compose version &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: 'docker compose' plugin not found." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if [ ! -f "$NT_FILE" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $NT_FILE not found." >&2
    echo "  Copy it from the source server or re-run the pipeline." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_up() {
  check_prereqs
  mkdir -p data/qlever-index data/shmarql-store

  echo "=== Starting QLever (port $QLEVER_PORT) ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml up -d --wait \
    --wait-timeout 600
  echo "  SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo ""

  echo "=== Starting SHMARQL (port $SHMARQL_PORT) ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml up -d
  echo "  SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:$SHMARQL_PORT/sparql"
  echo "  UI:              http://localhost:$SHMARQL_PORT/"
  echo ""

  echo "=== MCP server (Claude Code) ==="
  echo "  Run once to register:"
  echo "  claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \\"
  echo "    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest \\"
  echo "    -e http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
}

cmd_down() {
  echo "Stopping QLever..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml down
  echo "Stopping SHMARQL..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml down
}

cmd_status() {
  echo "=== QLever ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml ps
  echo ""
  echo "=== SHMARQL ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml ps
}

cmd_logs() {
  local service="${1:-}"
  if [ "$service" = "qlever" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml logs -f
  elif [ "$service" = "shmarql" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml logs -f
  else
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_mcp_add() {
  echo "Registering MCP server with Claude Code..."
  claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \
    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e "http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo "Done. Test with: claude mcp list"
}

case "${1:-}" in
  up)       cmd_up ;;
  down)     cmd_down ;;
  status)   cmd_status ;;
  logs)     cmd_logs "${2:-}" ;;
  mcp-add)  cmd_mcp_add ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh <command>"
    echo "  up        Start QLever + SHMARQL (builds index on first run)"
    echo "  down      Stop both services"
    echo "  status    Show container status"
    echo "  logs      ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    echo "  mcp-add   Register QLever as Claude Code MCP server"
    echo ""
    echo "Ports (override via env vars):"
    echo "  QLEVER_PORT=$QLEVER_PORT  (QLever SPARQL)"
    echo "  SHMARQL_PORT=$SHMARQL_PORT  (SHMARQL UI + SPARQL)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

content.thinking:
The user wants a mermaid diagram of the current setup. Let me think about what we have:

- Host machine
  - `goethe-faust/output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt` (1.3 GB source file)
  - `goethe-faust/data/qlever-index/` (persisted index)
  - `goethe-faust/data/shmarql-store/` (persisted store)
  - `goethe-faust/yasgui/index.html` (static UI files)

Docker services (docker-compose.qlever.yml):


- `qlever-goethe-faust` (adfreiburg/qlever:latest) - port 7030
- `yasgui` (nginx:alpine) - port 7031, serves yasgui/index.html

Docker services (docker-compose.shmarql.yml):
- `shmarql-goethe-faust` (ghcr.io/epoz/shmarql:latest) - port 8030

User/browser:
- Accesses YASGUI at 7031 → YASGUI sends SPARQL to QLever at 7030
- Accesses SHMARQL UI at 8030
- Claude Code MCP → docker

The MCP server for QLever will connect to the QLever instance running on port 7030. Let me sketch out a clean diagram to visualize all these connections.

content.text:
```mermaid
graph TB
    NT["📄 output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt\n1.3 GB · 8.6M triples"]

    subgraph compose-qlever["docker-compose.qlever.yml"]
        QLever["adfreiburg/qlever:latest\nSPARQL endpoint\n:7030"]
        YASGUI["nginx:alpine\nYASGUI\n:7031"]
        QIdx["💾 data/qlever-index/\nbinary index"]
    end

    subgraph compose-shmarql["docker-compose.shmarql.yml"]
        SHMARQL["ghcr.io/epoz/shmarql:latest\nSPARQL + UI\n:8030"]
        SStore["💾 data/shmarql-store/\npyoxigraph store"]
    end

    MCP["mcp-server-qlever\n(docker run, --network=host)"]
    Browser["🌐 Browser"]
    Claude["🤖 Claude Code"]

    NT -->|"mount :ro /input"| QLever
    QLever <-->|"bind mount"| QIdx
    NT -->|"mount :ro /data"| SHMARQL
    SHMARQL <-->|"bind mount"| SStore

    YASGUI -->|"depends_on healthy"| QLever
    YASGUI -->|"serves index.html"| Browser
    Browser -->|"SPARQL queries\nhttp://localhost:7030"| QLever
    Browser -->|"UI + SPARQL\nhttp://localhost:8030"| SHMARQL

    Claude -->|"MCP tools"| MCP
    MCP -->|"http://localhost:7030"| QLever
```
where can i have this rendered / displayed?

A few options:

1. **GitHub** — paste it in any `.md` file or issue/PR comment; renders natively.

2. **Mermaid Live Editor** — `mermaid.live` — paste and see it instantly; can export PNG/SVG.

3. **VS Code** — with the [Markdown Preview Mermaid Support](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=bierner.markdown-mermaid) extension, it renders in the built-in markdown preview.

4. **Obsidian** — renders Mermaid natively in preview mode, no plugin needed.

Quickest option if you just want to see it now: `mermaid.live`.

content.thinking:
The user wants me to open the Mermaid Live Editor with the diagram. I can construct a URL for mermaid.live that encodes the diagram. The mermaid.live editor accepts a base64-encoded JSON payload in the URL hash.

The format is: `https://mermaid.live/edit#base64:<encoded>`

The JSON structure is:
```json
{
  "code": "<mermaid code>",
  "mermaid": {"theme": "default"}
}
```

Then base64 encode that JSON and append to the URL.

Let me construct this. I'll encode the mermaid diagram.


Now I'm building the Mermaid diagram that shows the architecture with the N-Triples file, the QLever Docker Compose setup with the SPARQL endpoint and YASGUI interface, and the Shmarql Docker Compose configuration. I need to base64 encode this diagram, so I'll use a bash command to handle that.

input.command:
python3 -c "
import base64, json

code = '''graph TB
    NT[\"📄 output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt\\n1.3 GB · 8.6M triples\"]

    subgraph compose-qlever[\"docker-compose.qlever.yml\"]
        QLever[\"adfreiburg/qlever:latest\\nSPARQL endpoint\\n:7030\"]
        YASGUI[\"nginx:alpine\\nYASGUI\\n:7031\"]
        QIdx[\"💾 data/qlever-index/\\nbinary index\"]
    end

    subgraph compose-shmarql[\"docker-compose.shmarql.yml\"]
        SHMARQL[\"ghcr.io/epoz/shmarql:latest\\nSPARQL + UI\\n:8030\"]
        SStore[\"💾 data/shmarql-store/\\npyoxigraph store\"]
    end

    MCP[\"mcp-server-qlever\\n(docker run, --network=host)\"]
    Browser[\"🌐 Browser\"]
    Claude[\"🤖 Claude Code\"]

    NT -->|\"mount :ro /input\"| QLever
    QLever <-->|\"bind mount\"| QIdx
    NT -->|\"mount :ro /data\"| SHMARQL
    SHMARQL <-->|\"bind mount\"| SStore

    YASGUI -->|\"depends_on healthy\"| QLever
    YASGUI -->|\"serves index.html\"| Browser
    Browser -->|\"SPARQL queries\\nhttp://localhost:7030\"| QLever
    Browser -->|\"UI + SPARQL\\nhttp://localhost:8030\"| SHMARQL

    Claude -->|\"MCP tools\"| MCP
    MCP -->|\"http://localhost:7030\"| QLever'''

payload = json.dumps({'code': code, 'mermaid': {'theme': 'default'}})
encoded = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(payload.encode()).decode()
print('https://mermaid.live/edit#base64:' + encoded)
"

# Purpose:    Open the goethe-faust setup diagram in mermaid.live.

# Outputs:    mermaid.live URL (stdout) + browser open
# Dependencies: Python 3 stdlib only
# Assumptions: Internet access to mermaid.live

import base64
import json
import webbrowser

DIAGRAM = """\
graph TB
    NT["📄 output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt\\n1.3 GB · 8.6M triples"]

    subgraph compose-qlever["docker-compose.qlever.yml"]
        QLever["adfreiburg/qlever:latest\\nSPARQL endpoint\\n:7030"]
        YASGUI["nginx:alpine\\nYASGUI\\n:7031"]
        QIdx["💾 data/qlever-index/\\nbinary index"]
    end

    subgraph compose-shmarql["docker-compose.shmarql.yml"]
        SHMARQL["ghcr.io/epoz/shmarql:latest\\nSPARQL + UI\\n:8030"]
        SStore["💾 data/shmarql-store/\\npyoxigraph store"]
    end

    MCP["mcp-server-qlever\\n(docker run, --network=host)"]
    Browser["🌐 Browser"]
    Claude["🤖 Claude Code"]

    NT -->|"mount :ro /input"| QLever
    QLever <-->|"bind mount"| QIdx
    NT -->|"mount :ro /data"| SHMARQL
    SHMARQL <-->|"bind mount"| SStore

    YASGUI -->|"depends_on healthy"| QLever
    YASGUI -->|"serves index.html"| Browser
    Browser -->|"SPARQL queries\\nhttp://localhost:7030"| QLever
    Browser -->|"UI + SPARQL\\nhttp://localhost:8030"| SHMARQL

    Claude -->|"MCP tools"| MCP
    MCP -->|"http://localhost:7030"| QLever\
"""

payload = json.dumps({"code": DIAGRAM, "mermaid": {"theme": "default"}})
encoded = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(payload.encode()).decode()

import os, sqlite3, random, json, logging

log = logging.getLogger("SHMARQL")
handler = logging.StreamHandler()
log.addHandler(handler)


DEBUG = os.environ.get("DEBUG", "0") == "1"
if DEBUG:
    log.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
    handler.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
    formatter = logging.Formatter(
        "%(levelname)-9s %(name)s %(asctime)s %(message)s", datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"
    )
    handler.setFormatter(formatter)
    log.debug("Debug logging requested from config env DEBUG")
else:
    log.setLevel(logging.INFO)
    log.info("SHMARQL Logging at INFO level")


ENDPOINT = os.environ.get("ENDPOINT")

# ENDPOINTS variable with name|url pairs
ens = os.environ.get("ENDPOINTS", "")

# Split the string into name|url pairs and then further split each pair
ens_pairs = [pair.split("|") for pair in ens.split(" ") if "|" in pair]

# Convert into a dictionary
ENDPOINTS = {name: url for name, url in ens_pairs}

SCHEME = os.environ.get("SCHEME", "http://")
DOMAIN = os.environ.get("DOMAIN", "127.0.0.1")
PORT = os.environ.get("PORT", "5001")
# Note, we can't just build a SITE_URI from the above variables because the
# app might be running behind a reverse proxy
SITE_URI = os.environ.get("SITE_URI", "http://127.0.0.1:8000/")

# This is a mountpoint that will be prefixed to all URIs served by the application
MOUNT = os.environ.get("MOUNT", "/")

QUERIES_DB = os.environ.get("QUERIES_DB", "queries.db")
thequerydb = sqlite3.connect(QUERIES_DB)
thequerydb.executescript(
    """CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS queries (queryhash TEXT, query TEXT, timestamp TEXT, endpoint TEXT, result TEXT, duration FLOAT);
pragma journal_mode=WAL;"""
)

if "DATA_LOAD_PATHS" in os.environ:
    DATA_LOAD_PATHS = os.environ.get("DATA_LOAD_PATHS").split(" ")
else:
    DATA_LOAD_PATHS = []
STORE_PATH = os.environ.get("STORE_PATH")

BIKIDATA_DB = os.environ.get("BIKIDATA_DB")
SEMANTIC_INDEX = os.environ.get("SEMANTIC_INDEX", "0") == "1"
RDF2VEC_INDEX = os.environ.get("RDF2VEC_INDEX", "0") == "1"

FTS_FILEPATH = os.environ.get("FTS_FILEPATH")
RDF2VEC_FILEPATH = os.environ.get("RDF2VEC_FILEPATH")

if FTS_FILEPATH:
    log.exception(
        "FTS_FILEPATH is set, but this config has been removed. Please use BIKIDATA_DB instead. See https://shmarql.com/fizzysearch/ for more information."
    )
    FTS_FILEPATH = None
if RDF2VEC_FILEPATH:
    log.exception(
        "RDF2VEC_FILEPATH is set, but this config has been removed. Please use RDF2VEC_INDEX boolean and BIKIDATA_DB instead. See https://shmarql.com/rdf2vec/ for more information."
    )
    RDF2VEC_FILEPATH = None


SPARQL_QUERY_UI = os.environ.get("SPARQL_QUERY_UI", "1") == "1"

SITE_ID = os.environ.get(
    "SITE_ID", "".join([random.choice("abcdef0123456789") for _ in range(10)])
)

SITEDOCS_PATH = os.environ.get("SITEDOCS_PATH", os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "site"))
SCHPIEL_PATH = os.environ.get("SCHPIEL_PATH")

PREFIXES_FILEPATH = os.environ.get("PREFIXES_FILEPATH")
DEFAULT_PREFIXES = {
    "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#": "rdf:",
    "http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#": "rdfs:",
    "http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#": "owl:",
    "http://schema.org/": "schema:",
    "http://www.wikidata.org/entity/": "wd:",
    "http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/": "wds:",
    "http://wikiba.se/ontology#": "wikibase:",
    "http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/": "wdt:",
    "http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#": "skos:",
    "http://purl.org/dc/terms/": "dct:",
    "http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/": "dc:",
    "http://dbpedia.org/resource/": "dbr:",
    "https://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#": "rico:",
    "http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#": "geo:",
    "http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#": "sh:",
    "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#": "xsd:",
    "http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#": "virtrdfdata:",
    "http://www.openlinksw.com/schemas/virtrdf#": "virtrdf:",
    "http://purl.org/spar/fabio/": "fabio:",
    "http://www.w3.org/2003/11/swrl#": "swrl:",
    "http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#": "dcat:",
    "https://shmarql.com/": "shmarql:",
    "https://database.factgrid.de/entity/": "factgrid:",
}


def read_prefixes_from_ttl(filepath: str):
    if not os.path.exists(filepath):
        log.warning(f"PREFIXES_FILEPATH {filepath} does not exist.")
        return {}

    tmp_prefixes = {}
    for line in open(filepath).readlines():
        if not line.lower().startswith("@prefix "):
            continue
        if not line.lower().endswith(" .\n"):
            continue
        line = line.strip("\n .")
        parts = line.split(":")
        if len(parts) < 2:
            continue
        prefix = parts[0][8:] + ":"
        prefix_uri = ":".join(parts[1:]).strip("<> ")
        if prefix == ":":
            prefix = " "
        tmp_prefixes[prefix_uri] = prefix
    return tmp_prefixes


PREFIXES = DEFAULT_PREFIXES
try:
    if PREFIXES_FILEPATH:
        # also support reading the prefixed from a .ttl file for convenience
        if PREFIXES_FILEPATH.endswith(".ttl"):
            PREFIXES = DEFAULT_PREFIXES | read_prefixes_from_ttl(PREFIXES_FILEPATH)
        else:
            PREFIXES = DEFAULT_PREFIXES | json.load(open(PREFIXES_FILEPATH))
except:
    log.exception(f"Problem binding PREFIXES from {PREFIXES_FILEPATH}")

PREFIXES_SNIPPET = "".join(
    f"PREFIX {prefix} <{uri}>\n" for uri, prefix in PREFIXES.items()
)

if os.environ.get("NFDI_PREFIXES", "1") == "1":
    NFDI_PREFIXES_FILEPATH = os.path.join(
        os.path.dirname(__file__), "nfdi_prefixes.ttl"
    )
    PREFIXES = PREFIXES | read_prefixes_from_ttl(NFDI_PREFIXES_FILEPATH)

ADMIN_DATABASE = os.environ.get("ADMIN_DATABASE")

LOGINS = os.environ.get("LOGINS") == "1"

SITE_TITLE = os.environ.get("SITE_TITLE", "SHMARQL")

########## Config values that do not exist any more ##########

if os.environ.get("WATCH_DOCS"):
    log.warning(
        "WATCH_DOCS config has been removed. The automatic rebuild of mkdocs files is not supported any more. The system can now render mkdocs files on the fly."
    )

1→import httpx, logging, random, hashlib, json, time, sqlite3, os, gzip
     2→import fizzysearch
     3→from .config import (
     4→    ENDPOINT,
     5→    ENDPOINTS,
     6→    QUERIES_DB,
     7→    BIKIDATA_DB,
     8→    SEMANTIC_INDEX,
     9→    PREFIXES_SNIPPET,
    10→    DATA_LOAD_PATHS,
    11→    STORE_PATH,
    12→    log,
    13→)
    14→import pyoxigraph as px
    15→from .px_util import OxigraphSerialization, string_iterator
    16→
    17→
    18→def hash_query(query: str) -> str:
    19→    return hashlib.md5(query.encode("utf8")).hexdigest()
    20→
    21→
    22→def cached_query(query: str, endpoint: str = None):
    23→    # Only use the endpoint if specified
    24→    if endpoint:
    25→        theq = sqlite3.connect(QUERIES_DB).execute(
    26→            "SELECT timestamp, result, duration FROM queries WHERE queryhash = ? and endpoint = ? and not result is null ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 1",
    27→            (hash_query(query), endpoint),
    28→        )
    29→    else:
    30→        theq = sqlite3.connect(QUERIES_DB).execute(
    31→            "SELECT timestamp, result, duration FROM queries WHERE queryhash = ? and not result is null ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 1",
    32→            (hash_query(query),),
    33→        )
    34→
    35→    for timestamp, result, duration in theq:
    36→        result = json.loads(result)
    37→        result["timestamp"] = timestamp
    38→        result["duration"] = duration
    39→        result["cached"] = True
    40→        return result
    41→
    42→
    43→def parse_prefixes(querystring: str) -> dict:
    44→    prefixes = {}
    45→    for line in querystring.split("\n"):
    46→        if not line.lower().startswith("prefix "):
    47→            continue
    48→        if not line.lower().endswith(">"):
    49→            continue
    50→        parts = line.split(":")
    51→        if len(parts) < 2:
    52→            continue
    53→        prefix = parts[0][7:] + ":"
    54→        prefix_uri = ":".join(parts[1:]).strip("<> ")
    55→        if prefix == ":":
    56→            prefix = " "
    57→        prefixes[prefix_uri] = prefix
    58→    return prefixes
    59→
    60→
    61→def do_query(query: str) -> dict:
    62→    to_use = ENDPOINT
    63→
    64→    try:
    65→        rewritten = fizzysearch.rewrite(
    66→            query,
    67→            {
    68→                "https://fizzysearch.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/fts": fizzysearch.use_fts(),
    69→                "fizzy:fts": fizzysearch.use_fts(),
    70→                "fizzy:ftsStats": fizzysearch.use_fts_stats(),
    71→            },
    72→        )
    73→    except Exception as e:
    74→        log.exception(f"Problem with fizzysearch: {e}")
    75→        return {"error": f"Fizzysearch rewriting error: {e}"}
    76→
    77→    shmarql_settings = {}
    78→    for comment in rewritten["comments"]:
    79→        log.debug(f"fizzysearch SPARQL Comment: {comment}")
    80→        if comment.find("shmarql-engine:") > -1:
    81→            to_use = ENDPOINTS.get(comment.split(" ")[-1])
    82→        if comment.startswith("shmarql-"):
    83→            comment_value = [x.strip(" ") for x in comment[8:].split(":")]
    84→            if len(comment_value) > 1:
    85→                shmarql_settings.setdefault(comment_value[0], []).append(
    86→                    ":".join(comment_value[1:])
    87→                )
    88→
    89→    query = rewritten.get("rewritten", query)
    90→
    91→    if not to_use:
    92→        if len(ENDPOINTS) > 0:
    93→            to_use = random.choice(list(ENDPOINTS.values()))
    94→        elif len(GRAPH) > 0:
    95→            to_use = "__local__"
    96→        else:
    97→            return {"error": "No endpoint found"}
    98→
    99→    if not "nocache" in shmarql_settings:
   100→        cached_query_result = cached_query(query)
   101→        if cached_query_result:
   102→            return cached_query_result
   103→
   104→    time_start = time.time()
   105→    result = {}
   106→    if to_use == "__local__":
   107→        try:
   108→            qquery = PREFIXES_SNIPPET + "\n" + query
   109→            r = GRAPH.query(qquery, use_default_graph_as_union=True)
   110→            result = OxigraphSerialization(r).json()
   111→        except Exception as e:
   112→            return {"error": str(e)}
   113→    else:
   114→        if rewritten.get("query_type") == "construct":
   115→            accept_header = "text/turtle"
   116→        else:
   117→            accept_header = "application/sparql-results+json"
   118→        headers = {
   119→            "Accept": accept_header,
   120→            "User-Agent": "SHMARQL/2024 (https://shmarql.com/ ep@epoz.org)",
   121→        }
   122→
   123→        data = {
   124→            "query": PREFIXES_SNIPPET + "\n" + query,
   125→        }
   126→        try:
   127→            r = httpx.post(to_use, data=data, headers=headers, timeout=180)
   128→            if r.status_code == 200:
   129→                try:
   130→                    result = r.json()
   131→                except json.JSONDecodeError:
   132→                    result = {"data": r.content.decode("utf8")}
   133→            elif r.status_code == 500:
   134→                return {"error": r.text}
   135→        except:
   136→            log.exception(f"Problem with {to_use}")
   137→            return {"error": "Exception raised querying endpoint"}
   138→
   139→    time_end = time.time()
   140→    duration = time_end - time_start
   141→
   142→    # get the endpoint name from the to_use URL
   143→    endpoint_name = "default"
   144→    for k, v in ENDPOINTS.items():
   145→        if v == to_use:
   146→            endpoint_name = k
   147→
   148→    if result:
   149→        try:
   150→            result["prefixes"] = parse_prefixes(query)
   151→        except:
   152→            log.error("Error parsing prefixes from query")
   153→        result["duration"] = duration
   154→        result["endpoint_name"] = endpoint_name
   155→        result["endpoint"] = to_use
   156→        result["shmarql_settings"] = shmarql_settings
   157→
   158→        thequerydb = sqlite3.connect(QUERIES_DB)
   159→        thequerydb.execute(
   160→            "INSERT INTO queries (queryhash, query, timestamp, endpoint, result, duration) VALUES (?, ?, datetime(), ?, ?, ?)",
   161→            (hash_query(query), query, to_use, json.dumps(result), duration),
   162→        )
   163→        thequerydb.commit()
   164→
   165→        return result
   166→    else:
   167→        return {"error": r.text, "status": r.status_code}
   168→
   169→
   170→def initialize_graph(data_load_paths: list, store_path: str = None) -> px.Store:
   171→    log.debug(f"Initialize graph with configs: {data_load_paths} and {store_path}")
   172→    store_primary = True
   173→    if store_path:
   174→        log.debug(f"Opening store from {store_path}")
   175→        if data_load_paths:
   176→            # If there are multiple workers trying to load at the same time,
   177→            # contention for the lock will happen.
   178→            # Do a short wait to stagger start times and let one win, the rest will lock and open read_only
   179→            time.sleep(random.random() / 2)
   180→            try:
   181→                GRAPH = px.Store(store_path)
   182→                log.debug("This process won the loading contention")
   183→            except OSError:
   184→                log.debug("Secondary, opening store read-only")
   185→                GRAPH = px.Store.secondary(store_path)
   186→                store_primary = False
   187→        else:
   188→            log.debug("Opening store read-only")
   189→            GRAPH = px.Store.read_only(store_path)
   190→    else:
   191→        GRAPH = px.Store()
   192→
   193→    if len(GRAPH) < 1 and data_load_paths and store_primary:
   194→        for data_load_path in data_load_paths:
   195→            if data_load_path.startswith("http://") or data_load_path.startswith(
   196→                "https://"
   197→            ):
   198→                log.debug(f"Downloading {data_load_path}")
   199→                # Try downloading this file and parsing it as a string
   200→                start_download = time.time()
   201→                r = httpx.get(data_load_path, follow_redirects=True, timeout=180)
   202→                if r.status_code == 200:
   203→                    log.debug(
   204→                        f"Downloading {data_load_path} took {int(time.time() - start_download)} seconds"
   205→                    )
   206→                    d = r.content
   207→                    # Try and guess content type from extention, default is turtle
   208→                    # if .rdf or .nt use on of those
   209→                    if (
   210→                        data_load_path.endswith(".rdf")
   211→                        or data_load_path.endswith(".xml")
   212→                        or data_load_path.endswith(".owl")
   213→                    ):
   214→                        GRAPH.bulk_load(r.content, "application/rdf+xml", lenient=True)
   215→                    elif data_load_path.endswith(".nt") or data_load_path.endswith(
   216→                        ".nt.gz"
   217→                    ):
   218→                        GRAPH.bulk_load(
   219→                            r.content, "application/n-triples", lenient=True
   220→                        )
   221→                    else:
   222→                        GRAPH.bulk_load(r.content, "text/turtle", lenient=True)
   223→            else:
   224→                if load_file_to_graph(GRAPH, data_load_path):
   225→                    continue
   226→                for dirpath, _, filenames in os.walk(data_load_path):
   227→                    for filename in filenames:
   228→                        try:
   229→                            filepath = os.path.join(dirpath, filename)
   230→                            load_file_to_graph(GRAPH, filepath)
   231→                        except SyntaxError:
   232→                            log.error(f"Failed to parse {filepath}")
   233→
   234→    log.debug(f"Graph haz {len(GRAPH)} triples")
   235→
   236→    if store_primary and BIKIDATA_DB and not os.path.exists(BIKIDATA_DB):
   237→        r = fizzysearch.build_from_iterator(string_iterator(GRAPH))
   238→        log.debug(
   239→            f"NEW bikidata built at {BIKIDATA_DB} with {r.get('count', '?')} literals"
   240→        )
   241→
   242→    return GRAPH
   243→
   244→
   245→def load_file_to_graph(graph: px.Store, filepath: str) -> bool:
   246→    was_file = False
   247→    try:
   248→        filename = os.path.basename(filepath)
   249→        if filename.endswith(".gz"):
   250→            filepath = gzip.open(filepath)
   251→            filename = filename[:-3]
   252→        else:
   253→            filepath = open(filepath, "rb")
   254→        if filename.lower().endswith(".ttl"):
   255→            log.debug(f"Parsing {filepath} as ttl")
   256→            graph.bulk_load(filepath, px.RdfFormat.TURTLE, lenient=True)
   257→            was_file = True
   258→        elif filename.lower().endswith(".nt"):
   259→            log.debug(f"Parsing {filepath} as nt")
   260→            graph.bulk_load(filepath, px.RdfFormat.N_TRIPLES, lenient=True)
   261→            was_file = True
   262→        elif filename.lower().endswith(".trig"):
   263→            log.debug(f"Parsing {filepath} as trig")
   264→            graph.bulk_load(filepath, px.RdfFormat.TRIG, lenient=True)
   265→            was_file = True
   266→    except Exception as e:
   267→        log.debug(f"{filepath} exception {e}")
   268→    return was_file
   269→
   270→
   271→if not (ENDPOINT or len(ENDPOINTS) > 0):
   272→    log.debug("No ENDPOINT or ENDPOINTS defined, using local graph")
   273→    GRAPH = initialize_graph(DATA_LOAD_PATHS, STORE_PATH)
   274→

content.text:
Yes — and it's straightforward. The key is `qry.py:271–273`:

```python
if not (ENDPOINT or len(ENDPOINTS) > 0):
    # only then load pyoxigraph
    GRAPH = initialize_graph(DATA_LOAD_PATHS, STORE_PATH)
```

When `ENDPOINT` is set, SHMARQL skips pyoxigraph entirely and proxies all queries via `httpx.post` to the external endpoint (`qry.py:127`). It sends standard `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` POST with `query=...` and `Accept: application/sparql-results+json` — which QLever handles fine.

So to run SHMARQL as a UI/proxy in front of QLever, just set `ENDPOINT=http://qlever-goethe-faust:7019` and drop `DATA_LOAD_PATHS`/`STORE_PATH`:

```yaml
  shmarql-qlever:
    image: ghcr.io/epoz/shmarql:latest
    ports:
      - "8031:8000"
    environment:
      - ENDPOINT=http://qlever-goethe-faust:7019
    depends_on:
      qlever-goethe-faust:
        condition: service_healthy
```

This gives you SHMARQL's UI (linked data browsing, prefix resolution, query caching) with QLever's query performance. Want me to add this as a third service to `docker-compose.qlever.yml`?
yes

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# QLever triplestore for goethe-faust DDB EDM data.
#
# First run builds the index from the NT file (a few minutes for 8.6M triples).
# Subsequent runs start in seconds — index persisted in ./data/qlever-index/.
#
# To re-run on another server: copy this directory (including output/*.nt),
# then: docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml up -d --wait
#
# SPARQL UI:              http://localhost:7031
# QLever SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:7030
# MCP server (Claude Code):
#   claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \
#     ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e http://localhost:7030

services:
  qlever-goethe-faust:
    image: docker.io/adfreiburg/qlever:latest
    ports:
      - "${QLEVER_PORT:-7030}:7019"
    volumes:
      - ./output:/input:ro
      - ./data/qlever-index:/data
    working_dir: /data
    entrypoint: ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
    command:
      - |
        set -e

        if [ -f /data/index.ready ]; then
          echo "Index already built, starting server..."
          exec /qlever/qlever-server -i /data/goethe-faust -p 7019 \
            -m $${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB} --default-query-timeout 60s
        fi

        echo "=== Goethe-Faust Setup ==="
        echo "Step 1/2: Building QLever index from NT file (8.6M triples)..."

        cat > /data/settings.json << 'SETTINGS'
        {
          "num-triples-per-batch": 500000,
          "parser-batch-size": 50000,
          "ascii-prefixes-only": false,
          "languages-internal": ["de", "en", ""],
          "prefixes-external": [
            "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/",
            "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/organization/",
            "urn:edm:Aggregation:",
            "urn:edm:ProvidedCHO:",
            "http://www.deutschefotothek.de/documents/obj/",
            "http://fotothek.slub-dresden.de/",
            "http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/",
            "http://www.w3.org/"
          ]
        }
        SETTINGS

        /qlever/qlever-index -i /data/goethe-faust \
          -f /input/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt \
          -F nt \
          -s /data/settings.json 2>&1

        touch /data/index.ready
        echo "Step 2/2: Index built."

        echo "=== Goethe-Faust QLever ready ==="
        exec /qlever/qlever-server -i /data/goethe-faust -p 7019 \
          -m $${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB} --default-query-timeout 60s
    environment:
      - QLEVER_MEMORY=${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB}
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-sf", "http://localhost:7019/?cmd=stats"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 120
      start_period: 300s

  yasgui:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
      - "${YASGUI_PORT:-7031}:80"
    volumes:
      - ./yasgui:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro
    depends_on:
      qlever-goethe-faust:
        condition: service_healthy

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Purpose:    Start/stop QLever and SHMARQL for the goethe-faust DDB EDM
#             dataset.
# Usage:      ./setup.sh <command>
#             Commands: up | down | status | logs | mcp-add
# Inputs:     output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt (1.3 GB, 8.6M triples)
# Outputs:    data/qlever-index/  — QLever binary index (persisted)
#             data/shmarql-store/ — pyoxigraph store (persisted)
# Dependencies: docker, docker compose
# Assumptions: Run from the goethe-faust/ directory.
#              On a new server, copy the full directory (including output/*.nt)
#              then run this script.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

NT_FILE="output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt"
QLEVER_PORT="${QLEVER_PORT:-7030}"
YASGUI_PORT="${YASGUI_PORT:-7031}"
SHMARQL_PORT="${SHMARQL_PORT:-8030}"

check_prereqs() {
  if ! command -v docker &>/dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: docker not found. Install Docker Desktop or Docker Engine." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if ! docker compose version &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: 'docker compose' plugin not found." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if [ ! -f "$NT_FILE" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $NT_FILE not found." >&2
    echo "  Copy it from the source server or re-run the pipeline." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_up() {
  check_prereqs
  mkdir -p data/qlever-index data/shmarql-store

  echo "=== Starting QLever + YASGUI ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml up -d --wait \
    --wait-timeout 600
  echo "  SPARQL UI:       http://localhost:$YASGUI_PORT"
  echo "  SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo ""

  echo "=== Starting SHMARQL (port $SHMARQL_PORT) ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml up -d
  echo "  SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:$SHMARQL_PORT/sparql"
  echo "  UI:              http://localhost:$SHMARQL_PORT/"
  echo ""

  echo "=== MCP server (Claude Code) ==="
  echo "  Run once to register:"
  echo "  claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \\"
  echo "    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest \\"
  echo "    -e http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
}

cmd_down() {
  echo "Stopping QLever..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml down
  echo "Stopping SHMARQL..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml down
}

cmd_status() {
  echo "=== QLever ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml ps
  echo ""
  echo "=== SHMARQL ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml ps
}

cmd_logs() {
  local service="${1:-}"
  if [ "$service" = "qlever" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml logs -f
  elif [ "$service" = "shmarql" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml logs -f
  else
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_mcp_add() {
  echo "Registering MCP server with Claude Code..."
  claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \
    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e "http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo "Done. Test with: claude mcp list"
}

case "${1:-}" in
  up)       cmd_up ;;
  down)     cmd_down ;;
  status)   cmd_status ;;
  logs)     cmd_logs "${2:-}" ;;
  mcp-add)  cmd_mcp_add ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh <command>"
    echo "  up        Start QLever + SHMARQL (builds index on first run)"
    echo "  down      Stop both services"
    echo "  status    Show container status"
    echo "  logs      ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    echo "  mcp-add   Register QLever as Claude Code MCP server"
    echo ""
    echo "Ports (override via env vars):"
    echo "  QLEVER_PORT=$QLEVER_PORT  (QLever SPARQL)"
    echo "  SHMARQL_PORT=$SHMARQL_PORT  (SHMARQL UI + SPARQL)"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Purpose:    Start/stop QLever and SHMARQL for the goethe-faust DDB EDM
#             dataset.
# Usage:      ./setup.sh <command>
#             Commands: up | down | status | logs | mcp-add
# Inputs:     output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt (1.3 GB, 8.6M triples)
# Outputs:    data/qlever-index/  — QLever binary index (persisted)
#             data/shmarql-store/ — pyoxigraph store (persisted)
# Dependencies: docker, docker compose
# Assumptions: Run from the goethe-faust/ directory.
#              On a new server, copy the full directory (including output/*.nt)
#              then run this script.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

NT_FILE="output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt"
QLEVER_PORT="${QLEVER_PORT:-7030}"
YASGUI_PORT="${YASGUI_PORT:-7031}"
SHMARQL_QLEVER_PORT="${SHMARQL_PORT:-7032}"
SHMARQL_PORT="${SHMARQL_PORT:-8030}"

check_prereqs() {
  if ! command -v docker &>/dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: docker not found. Install Docker Desktop or Docker Engine." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if ! docker compose version &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: 'docker compose' plugin not found." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if [ ! -f "$NT_FILE" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $NT_FILE not found." >&2
    echo "  Copy it from the source server or re-run the pipeline." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_up() {
  check_prereqs
  mkdir -p data/qlever-index data/shmarql-store

  echo "=== Starting QLever + YASGUI ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml up -d --wait \
    --wait-timeout 600
  echo "  SPARQL UI:       http://localhost:$YASGUI_PORT"
  echo "  SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo ""

  echo "=== Starting SHMARQL (port $SHMARQL_PORT) ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml up -d
  echo "  SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:$SHMARQL_PORT/sparql"
  echo "  UI:              http://localhost:$SHMARQL_PORT/"
  echo ""

  echo "=== MCP server (Claude Code) ==="
  echo "  Run once to register:"
  echo "  claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \\"
  echo "    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest \\"
  echo "    -e http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
}

cmd_down() {
  echo "Stopping QLever..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml down
  echo "Stopping SHMARQL..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml down
}

cmd_status() {
  echo "=== QLever ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml ps
  echo ""
  echo "=== SHMARQL ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml ps
}

cmd_logs() {
  local service="${1:-}"
  if [ "$service" = "qlever" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml logs -f
  elif [ "$service" = "shmarql" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml logs -f
  else
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_mcp_add() {
  echo "Registering MCP server with Claude Code..."
  claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \
    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e "http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo "Done. Test with: claude mcp list"
}

case "${1:-}" in
  up)       cmd_up ;;
  down)     cmd_down ;;
  status)   cmd_status ;;
  logs)     cmd_logs "${2:-}" ;;
  mcp-add)  cmd_mcp_add ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh <command>"
    echo "  up        Start QLever + SHMARQL (builds index on first run)"
    echo "  down      Stop both services"
    echo "  status    Show container status"
    echo "  logs      ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    echo "  mcp-add   Register QLever as Claude Code MCP server"
    echo ""
    echo "Ports (override via env vars):"
    echo "  QLEVER_PORT=$QLEVER_PORT  (QLever SPARQL)"
    echo "  SHMARQL_PORT=$SHMARQL_PORT  (SHMARQL UI + SPARQL)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Purpose:    Open the goethe-faust setup diagram in mermaid.live.
# Usage:      python3 scripts/open_diagram.py
#             Prints the URL and opens it in the default browser.
# Inputs:     Inline Mermaid diagram string
# Outputs:    mermaid.live URL (stdout) + browser open
# Dependencies: Python 3 stdlib only
# Assumptions: Internet access to mermaid.live

import base64
import json
import webbrowser

DIAGRAM = """\
graph TB
    NT["📄 output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt\\n1.3 GB · 8.6M triples"]

    subgraph compose-qlever["docker-compose.qlever.yml"]
        QLever["adfreiburg/qlever:latest\\nSPARQL endpoint\\n:7030"]
        YASGUI["nginx:alpine\\nYASGUI\\n:7031"]
        QIdx["💾 data/qlever-index/\\nbinary index"]
    end

    subgraph compose-shmarql["docker-compose.shmarql.yml"]
        SHMARQL["ghcr.io/epoz/shmarql:latest\\nSPARQL + UI\\n:8030"]
        SStore["💾 data/shmarql-store/\\npyoxigraph store"]
    end

    MCP["mcp-server-qlever\\n(docker run, --network=host)"]
    Browser["🌐 Browser"]
    Claude["🤖 Claude Code"]

    NT -->|"mount :ro /input"| QLever
    QLever <-->|"bind mount"| QIdx
    NT -->|"mount :ro /data"| SHMARQL
    SHMARQL <-->|"bind mount"| SStore

    YASGUI -->|"depends_on healthy"| QLever
    YASGUI -->|"serves index.html"| Browser
    Browser -->|"SPARQL queries\\nhttp://localhost:7030"| QLever
    Browser -->|"UI + SPARQL\\nhttp://localhost:8030"| SHMARQL

    Claude -->|"MCP tools"| MCP
    MCP -->|"http://localhost:7030"| QLever\
"""

payload = json.dumps({"code": DIAGRAM, "mermaid": {"theme": "default"}})
encoded = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(payload.encode()).decode()
url = "https://mermaid.live/edit#base64:" + encoded

print(url)
webbrowser.open(url)

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Purpose:    Start/stop QLever and SHMARQL for the goethe-faust DDB EDM
#             dataset.
# Usage:      ./setup.sh <command>
#             Commands: up | down | status | logs | mcp-add
# Inputs:     output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt (1.3 GB, 8.6M triples)
# Outputs:    data/qlever-index/  — QLever binary index (persisted)
#             data/shmarql-store/ — pyoxigraph store (persisted)
# Dependencies: docker, docker compose
# Assumptions: Run from the goethe-faust/ directory.
#              On a new server, copy the full directory (including output/*.nt)
#              then run this script.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

NT_FILE="output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt"
QLEVER_PORT="${QLEVER_PORT:-7030}"
YASGUI_PORT="${YASGUI_PORT:-7031}"
SHMARQL_QLEVER_PORT="${SHMARQL_PORT:-7032}"
SHMARQL_PORT="${SHMARQL_PORT:-8030}"

check_prereqs() {
  if ! command -v docker &>/dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: docker not found. Install Docker Desktop or Docker Engine." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if ! docker compose version &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: 'docker compose' plugin not found." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if [ ! -f "$NT_FILE" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $NT_FILE not found." >&2
    echo "  Copy it from the source server or re-run the pipeline." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_up() {
  check_prereqs
  mkdir -p data/qlever-index data/shmarql-store

  echo "=== Starting QLever + YASGUI + SHMARQL ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml up -d --wait \
    --wait-timeout 600
  echo "  SPARQL UI (YASGUI):   http://localhost:$YASGUI_PORT"
  echo "  SPARQL UI (SHMARQL):  http://localhost:$SHMARQL_QLEVER_PORT"
  echo "  SPARQL endpoint:      http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo ""

  echo "=== Starting SHMARQL (port $SHMARQL_PORT) ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml up -d
  echo "  SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:$SHMARQL_PORT/sparql"
  echo "  UI:              http://localhost:$SHMARQL_PORT/"
  echo ""

  echo "=== MCP server (Claude Code) ==="
  echo "  Run once to register:"
  echo "  claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \\"
  echo "    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest \\"
  echo "    -e http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
}

cmd_down() {
  echo "Stopping QLever..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml down
  echo "Stopping SHMARQL..."
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml down
}

cmd_status() {
  echo "=== QLever ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml ps
  echo ""
  echo "=== SHMARQL ==="
  docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml ps
}

cmd_logs() {
  local service="${1:-}"
  if [ "$service" = "qlever" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml logs -f
  elif [ "$service" = "shmarql" ]; then
    docker compose -f docker-compose.shmarql.yml logs -f
  else
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_mcp_add() {
  echo "Registering MCP server with Claude Code..."
  claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \
    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e "http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo "Done. Test with: claude mcp list"
}

case "${1:-}" in
  up)       cmd_up ;;
  down)     cmd_down ;;
  status)   cmd_status ;;
  logs)     cmd_logs "${2:-}" ;;
  mcp-add)  cmd_mcp_add ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh <command>"
    echo "  up        Start QLever + SHMARQL (builds index on first run)"
    echo "  down      Stop both services"
    echo "  status    Show container status"
    echo "  logs      ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    echo "  mcp-add   Register QLever as Claude Code MCP server"
    echo ""
    echo "Ports (override via env vars):"
    echo "  QLEVER_PORT=$QLEVER_PORT  (QLever SPARQL)"
    echo "  SHMARQL_PORT=$SHMARQL_PORT  (SHMARQL UI + SPARQL)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

input.content:
# Goethe-Faust — SPARQL Setup

DDB EDM dataset (8.6M triples) served via QLever, with SHMARQL as UI
and `mcp-server-qlever` for Claude Code access.

## Prerequisites

- Docker + Docker Compose plugin
- `output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt` (1.3 GB) — copy from source or
  re-run the pipeline to generate it

## Start

```bash
./setup.sh up
```

First run builds the QLever index (~few minutes). Subsequent runs
start in seconds — the index is persisted in `data/qlever-index/`.

## Services

| Service | URL | Description |
|---------|-----|-------------|
| SHMARQL | http://localhost:7032 | UI + SPARQL, backed by QLever |
| QLever  | http://localhost:7030 | Raw SPARQL endpoint |

Both are defined in `docker-compose.qlever.yml`.

## MCP (Claude Code)

Register once after `./setup.sh up`:

```bash
./setup.sh mcp-add
```

Then use QLever directly from Claude Code via the `goethe-faust` MCP
server.

## Other commands

```bash
./setup.sh down              # stop all services
./setup.sh status            # show container status
./setup.sh logs qlever       # tail QLever logs
./setup.sh logs shmarql      # tail SHMARQL logs
```

## Port overrides

```bash
QLEVER_PORT=7040 SHMARQL_PORT=7042 ./setup.sh up
```

## Transferring to another server

1. Copy this directory (including `output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt`)
2. Run `./setup.sh up`

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Purpose:    Open the goethe-faust setup diagram in mermaid.live.
# Usage:      python3 scripts/open_diagram.py
#             Prints the URL and opens it in the default browser.
# Inputs:     Inline Mermaid diagram string
# Outputs:    mermaid.live URL (stdout) + browser open
# Dependencies: Python 3 stdlib only
# Assumptions: Internet access to mermaid.live

import base64
import json
import webbrowser

DIAGRAM = """\
graph TB
    NT["📄 output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt\\n1.3 GB · 8.6M triples"]

    subgraph compose-qlever["docker-compose.qlever.yml"]
        QLever["adfreiburg/qlever:latest\\nSPARQL endpoint\\n:7030"]
        YASGUI["nginx:alpine\\nYASGUI\\n:7031"]
        SHMARQLq["ghcr.io/epoz/shmarql:latest\\nUI → QLever proxy\\n:7032"]
        QIdx["💾 data/qlever-index/\\nbinary index"]
    end

    subgraph compose-shmarql["docker-compose.shmarql.yml"]
        SHMARQL["ghcr.io/epoz/shmarql:latest\\nSPARQL + UI\\n:8030"]
        SStore["💾 data/shmarql-store/\\npyoxigraph store"]
    end

    MCP["mcp-server-qlever\\n(docker run, --network=host)"]
    Browser["🌐 Browser"]
    Claude["🤖 Claude Code"]

    NT -->|"mount :ro /input"| QLever
    QLever <-->|"bind mount"| QIdx
    NT -->|"mount :ro /data"| SHMARQL
    SHMARQL <-->|"bind mount"| SStore

    YASGUI -->|"depends_on healthy"| QLever
    SHMARQLq -->|"depends_on healthy"| QLever
    SHMARQLq -->|"ENDPOINT=http://qlever:7019"| QLever

    Browser -->|":7031"| YASGUI
    Browser -->|":7032"| SHMARQLq
    Browser -->|":7030 SPARQL"| QLever
    Browser -->|":8030"| SHMARQL

    Claude -->|"MCP tools"| MCP
    MCP -->|"http://localhost:7030"| QLever\
"""

payload = json.dumps({"code": DIAGRAM, "mermaid": {"theme": "default"}})
encoded = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(payload.encode()).decode()
url = "https://mermaid.live/edit#base64:" + encoded

print(url)
webbrowser.open(url)

# QLever triplestore for goethe-faust DDB EDM data.
#
# First run builds the index from the NT file (a few minutes for 8.6M triples).
# Subsequent runs start in seconds — index persisted in ./data/qlever-index/.
#
# To re-run on another server: copy this directory (including output/*.nt),
# then: docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml up -d --wait
#
# SPARQL UI (YASGUI):     http://localhost:7031
# SPARQL UI (SHMARQL):   http://localhost:7032
# QLever SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:7030
# MCP server (Claude Code):
#   claude mcp add goethe-faust -- docker run --rm -i --network=host \
#     ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest -e http://localhost:7030

services:
  qlever-goethe-faust:
    image: docker.io/adfreiburg/qlever:latest
    ports:
      - "${QLEVER_PORT:-7030}:7019"
    volumes:
      - ./output:/input:ro
      - ./data/qlever-index:/data
    working_dir: /data
    entrypoint: ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
    command:
      - |
        set -e

        if [ -f /data/index.ready ]; then
          echo "Index already built, starting server..."
          exec /qlever/qlever-server -i /data/goethe-faust -p 7019 \
            -m $${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB} --default-query-timeout 60s
        fi

        echo "=== Goethe-Faust Setup ==="
        echo "Step 1/2: Building QLever index from NT file (8.6M triples)..."

        cat > /data/settings.json << 'SETTINGS'
        {
          "num-triples-per-batch": 500000,
          "parser-batch-size": 50000,
          "ascii-prefixes-only": false,
          "languages-internal": ["de", "en", ""],
          "prefixes-external": [
            "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/",
            "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/organization/",
            "urn:edm:Aggregation:",
            "urn:edm:ProvidedCHO:",
            "http://www.deutschefotothek.de/documents/obj/",
            "http://fotothek.slub-dresden.de/",
            "http://www.europeana.eu/schemas/edm/",
            "http://www.w3.org/"
          ]
        }
        SETTINGS

        /qlever/qlever-index -i /data/goethe-faust \
          -f /input/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt \
          -F nt \
          -s /data/settings.json 2>&1

        touch /data/index.ready
        echo "Step 2/2: Index built."

        echo "=== Goethe-Faust QLever ready ==="
        exec /qlever/qlever-server -i /data/goethe-faust -p 7019 \
          -m $${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB} --default-query-timeout 60s
    environment:
      - QLEVER_MEMORY=${QLEVER_MEMORY:-4GB}
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-sf", "http://localhost:7019/?cmd=stats"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 120
      start_period: 300s

  yasgui:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
      - "${YASGUI_PORT:-7031}:80"
    volumes:
      - ./yasgui:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro
    depends_on:
      qlever-goethe-faust:
        condition: service_healthy

  shmarql:
    image: ghcr.io/epoz/shmarql:latest
    ports:
      - "${SHMARQL_PORT:-7032}:8000"
    environment:
      - ENDPOINT=http://qlever-goethe-faust:7019
    depends_on:
      qlever-goethe-faust:
        condition: service_healthy

Now I have a full picture. Let me plan the changes needed.

The user wants to be able to configure:
1. SHMARQL port
2. QLever port
3. Input NT file or directory of .nt files
4. Logs directory
5. Other artifacts (index directory, etc.)

Currently:
- Ports are partially configurable via env vars (`QLEVER_PORT`, `SHMARQL_PORT`) in docker-compose
- Input NT is hardcoded as `./output:/input:ro` and `/input/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt` in the qlever command


- Index directory is hardcoded as `./data/qlever-index`
- Logs directory doesn't exist yet

The main issues are that the NT filename is hardcoded in the qlever command, the system needs to handle multiple NT files when a directory is provided, and logs need a proper directory. Using a `.env` file with a `config.env` template would be the cleanest approach since docker compose automatically picks it up.

For handling NT files, QLever's `-f` flag can accept multiple files separated by spaces or use glob patterns like `*.nt`, and there's also a `--cat-input-files` option to concatenate them if needed.

For logging, the simplest approach is to capture Docker logs at the setup.sh level or mount a dedicated log volume, since both QLever and SHMARQL write their own logs that we'd want to preserve. to stdout/stderr, Docker captures these. A "logs directory" could mean:
- A local directory where `docker compose logs` output is written
- Or container log file mounts

I think the user means the Docker logs should be persisted to a directory on the host. The simplest approach: add logging configuration to docker-compose using the `json-file` driver with `--log-driver` and `--log-opt` pointing to a specific directory. But Docker's json-file driver stores logs at `/var/lib/docker/containers/...` which isn't easily customizable.

A practical approach: use a `LOG_DIR` env var and in `setup.sh`'s `cmd_logs`, redirect to `$LOG_DIR/qlever.log` and `$LOG_DIR/shmarql.log`.

For the configuration, the cleanest approach is a `.env` file auto-loaded by docker compose, a `config.env.example` template showing all options, and `setup.sh` reading from `.env` if it exists with sensible defaults.

Now I need to plan out the actual changes. I'll add variables for `NT_INPUT` (defaulting to the Goethe Faust file), `LOG_DIR`, `INDEX_DIR`, `QLEVER_MEMORY`, and keep the existing port variables. The `setup.sh` script should load these from `.env` when available, and `cmd_up` will handle writing or exporting the configuration.

For docker-compose, I need to handle the tricky case where `NT_INPUT` could be either a file or directory—I'll mount the parent directory and use a pattern variable for the container to know which files to process.

Looking at the qlever-index command more closely, it takes individual `-f` flags for each file, so I'll need to expand the glob pattern in the container script to pass multiple files properly.

The plan is to set up a `.env` file for user-editable configuration...

Continuing with the setup script...

I'm realizing the index name is hardcoded as `goethe-faust`, which should probably be configurable too—I'll add an `INDEX_NAME` variable with that as the default. The `index.ready` sentinel file lives in the data volume, so it'll work fine regardless of where `INDEX_DIR` points. Now I need to think through what other artifacts get created during indexing and how to handle them.

For the directories, I'm looking at setting up `INDEX_DIR` (defaulting to `data/qlever-index/`) and `LOG_DIR` (defaulting to `data/logs/`), since we're dropping the SHMARQL store entirely. The logging approach is straightforward—`LOG_DIR` becomes the target for both the `cmd_logs` command and any background Docker log captures, so I'll just wire it into the tee command.

Now I'm thinking through how to handle environment variables across `setup.sh` and Docker Compose. Docker Compose automatically loads `.env` from the compose file's directory, so I could put everything there and have both scripts access it. The tricky part is that `setup.sh` needs to compute `NT_INPUT_DIR` and `NT_INPUT_GLOB` from the base `NT_INPUT` value, and those derived variables need to reach Docker Compose. Since Docker Compose only reads the `.env` file itself (not shell exports), I'll need to write a separate `.env.docker` file with the computed values and pass it explicitly with `--env-file`.

Actually, wait — Docker Compose does inherit environment variables from the shell, so I can just export the computed values before calling `docker compose` and it'll pick them up. That's simpler than creating an extra file.

input.content:
# Plan: Configurable setup.sh

## Context
`setup.sh` and `docker-compose.qlever.yml` have several hardcoded values
(NT filename, index dir, ports). The user wants these to be configurable so
the stack can be reused for different datasets or deployed on different servers
without editing source files.

## Approach

### 1. `config.env` — user-editable config file (gitignored)
A single file the user edits before running `./setup.sh up`. Loaded by
`setup.sh` (via `source`) and by docker compose (which auto-loads `.env` —
we'll name it `config.env` and pass it explicitly with `--env-file`).

```
# Ports
QLEVER_PORT=7030
SHMARQL_PORT=7032

# Input: path to a single .nt file OR a directory of .nt files
NT_INPUT=output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt

# Directories (relative to goethe-faust/)
INDEX_DIR=data/qlever-index
LOG_DIR=data/logs

# QLever tuning
QLEVER_MEMORY=4GB
INDEX_NAME=goethe-faust
```

Add `config.env` to `.gitignore`; ship `config.env.example` as the template.

### 2. `setup.sh` changes

- Source `config.env` if it exists, otherwise fall back to defaults
- After sourcing, compute two derived vars from `NT_INPUT`:
  ```bash
  if [ -d "$NT_INPUT" ]; then
    NT_INPUT_DIR="$(realpath "$NT_INPUT")"
    NT_INPUT_GLOB="*.nt"
  else
    NT_INPUT_DIR="$(realpath "$(dirname "$NT_INPUT")")"
    NT_INPUT_GLOB="$(basename "$NT_INPUT")"
  fi
  export NT_INPUT_DIR NT_INPUT_GLOB
  ```
  These are exported so docker compose inherits them from the shell environment.
- Pass `--env-file config.env` (if it exists) to all `docker compose` calls
- `mkdir -p "$INDEX_DIR" "$LOG_DIR"`
- `cmd_logs`: tee docker compose logs to `$LOG_DIR/{qlever,shmarql}.log`
- Remove references to pyoxigraph SHMARQL compose file (already dead code)
- Remove `YASGUI_PORT` and `SHMARQL_QLEVER_PORT` (consolidated into `SHMARQL_PORT`)

### 3. `docker-compose.qlever.yml` changes

- `qlever-goethe-faust` service:
  - Volume: `${NT_INPUT_DIR}:/input:ro` (was hardcoded `./output:/input:ro`)
  - Volume: `${INDEX_DIR}:/data` (was hardcoded `./data/qlever-index:/data`)
  - Env var: `NT_INPUT_GLOB` passed into container
  - Env var: `INDEX_NAME` passed into container
  - Container script: replace hardcoded filename and index name with vars:
    ```bash
    FILES=$(ls /input/$NT_INPUT_GLOB 2>/dev/null | tr '\n' ' ')
    /qlever/qlever-index -i /data/$INDEX_NAME -f $FILES -F nt -s /data/settings.json
    exec /qlever/qlever-server -i /data/$INDEX_NAME ...
    ```
  - `index.ready` sentinel path: `/data/$INDEX_NAME.ready`
    (so different index names don't collide)
- `shmarql` service:
  - Port: `${SHMARQL_PORT:-7032}:8000` (already correct)
- Remove `yasgui` service
- Update header comment to remove YASGUI line

### 4. `config.env.example`
New file shipped in the repo showing all knobs with their defaults and
a one-line comment for each.

## Files to modify
- `setup.sh`
- `docker-compose.qlever.yml`

## Files to create
- `config.env.example`

## Files to gitignore
- `config.env` (add to `.gitignore` if not already present)

## Verification
1. `./setup.sh up` with no `config.env` → uses all defaults, works as before
2. Copy `config.env.example` → `config.env`, change `SHMARQL_PORT=7099`,
   run `./setup.sh up` → SHMARQL appears on 7099
3. Set `NT_INPUT=output/` (directory) → QLever indexes all `.nt` files in that dir
4. `./setup.sh logs qlever` → output written to `data/logs/qlever.log`
5. `./setup.sh status` → shows running containers

input.content:
# Plan: Configurable setup.sh

## Context
`setup.sh` and `docker-compose.qlever.yml` have hardcoded values (NT filename,
index directory, ports). This plan makes them configurable via a single
`config.env` file so the stack can be reused for different datasets and
deployed on other servers without editing source files.

## Config file: `config.env`

User-editable, gitignored. Sourced by `setup.sh` and passed to docker compose
via `--env-file`. Ship `config.env.example` as the committed template.

```bash
QLEVER_PORT=7030          # QLever raw SPARQL endpoint
SHMARQL_PORT=7032         # SHMARQL UI + SPARQL
NT_INPUT=output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt  # file or directory of .nt files
INDEX_DIR=data/qlever-index             # QLever binary index (persisted)
LOG_DIR=data/logs                       # log output directory
QLEVER_MEMORY=4GB         # memory cap for QLever server
INDEX_NAME=goethe-faust   # base name for QLever index files
```

## Changes: `setup.sh`

1. Source `config.env` if present; otherwise use inline defaults.
2. Compute `NT_INPUT_DIR` and `NT_INPUT_GLOB` from `NT_INPUT`:
   ```bash
   if [ -d "$NT_INPUT" ]; then
     NT_INPUT_DIR="$(realpath "$NT_INPUT")"
     NT_INPUT_GLOB="*.nt"
   else
     NT_INPUT_DIR="$(realpath "$(dirname "$NT_INPUT")")"
     NT_INPUT_GLOB="$(basename "$NT_INPUT")"
   fi
   export NT_INPUT_DIR NT_INPUT_GLOB INDEX_DIR INDEX_NAME
   ```
3. Pass `--env-file config.env` to all `docker compose` calls (when file exists).
4. `mkdir -p "$INDEX_DIR" "$LOG_DIR"` in `cmd_up`.
5. `cmd_logs`: tee docker compose logs to `$LOG_DIR/qlever.log` /
   `$LOG_DIR/shmarql.log`.
6. Remove dead references to pyoxigraph SHMARQL compose file and YASGUI.

## Changes: `docker-compose.qlever.yml`

- Volume mounts:
  - `${NT_INPUT_DIR}:/input:ro` (was `./output:/input:ro`)
  - `${INDEX_DIR}:/data` (was `./data/qlever-index:/data`)
- Pass env vars into container: `NT_INPUT_GLOB`, `INDEX_NAME`
- Container script:
  ```bash
  FILES=$(ls /input/$NT_INPUT_GLOB 2>/dev/null | tr '\n' ' ')
  /qlever/qlever-index -i /data/$INDEX_NAME -f $FILES -F nt -s /data/settings.json
  exec /qlever/qlever-server -i /data/$INDEX_NAME ...
  ```
- `index.ready` sentinel: `/data/$INDEX_NAME.ready`
  (keyed to index name so different datasets don't collide)
- Remove `yasgui` service.
- Update header comment.

## New file: `config.env.example`

Committed template; user copies to `config.env` and edits.

## `.gitignore`

Add `config.env` if not already present.

## Files

| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| Modify | `setup.sh` |
| Modify | `docker-compose.qlever.yml` |
| Create | `config.env.example` |
| Gitignore | `config.env` |

## Verification

1. No `config.env` → `./setup.sh up` uses defaults, works as before.
2. `SHMARQL_PORT=7099` in `config.env` → SHMARQL appears on 7099.
3. `NT_INPUT=output/` (directory) → QLever indexes all `.nt` files in dir.
4. `./setup.sh logs qlever` → output written to `data/logs/qlever.log`.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Purpose:    Start/stop QLever + SHMARQL for an RDF dataset.
# Usage:      ./setup.sh <command>
#             Commands: up | down | status | logs | mcp-add
# Inputs:     config.env (optional, copy from config.env.example)
#             NT_INPUT: .nt file or directory of .nt files
# Outputs:    $INDEX_DIR — QLever binary index (persisted)
#             $LOG_DIR   — log files from docker compose
# Dependencies: docker, docker compose
# Assumptions: Run from the goethe-faust/ directory.
#              On a new server, copy the directory and run ./setup.sh up.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

# --- Defaults (overridden by config.env) ---
QLEVER_PORT=7030
SHMARQL_PORT=7032
NT_INPUT=output/ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt
INDEX_DIR=data/qlever-index
LOG_DIR=data/logs
QLEVER_MEMORY=4GB
INDEX_NAME=goethe-faust

# Load config.env if present
if [ -f config.env ]; then
  # shellcheck disable=SC1091
  set -a; source config.env; set +a
fi

# Resolve NT_INPUT to absolute path
NT_INPUT="$(realpath "$NT_INPUT" 2>/dev/null || echo "$NT_INPUT")"

# Derive NT_INPUT_DIR and NT_INPUT_GLOB for docker compose
if [ -d "$NT_INPUT" ]; then
  NT_INPUT_DIR="$NT_INPUT"
  NT_INPUT_GLOB="*.nt"
else
  NT_INPUT_DIR="$(dirname "$NT_INPUT")"
  NT_INPUT_GLOB="$(basename "$NT_INPUT")"
fi

export NT_INPUT_DIR NT_INPUT_GLOB INDEX_DIR INDEX_NAME \
  QLEVER_PORT SHMARQL_PORT QLEVER_MEMORY

# Build --env-file flag if config.env exists
ENV_FILE_FLAG=""
if [ -f config.env ]; then
  ENV_FILE_FLAG="--env-file config.env"
fi

compose() {
  # shellcheck disable=SC2086
  docker compose -f docker-compose.qlever.yml $ENV_FILE_FLAG "$@"
}

check_prereqs() {
  if ! command -v docker &>/dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: docker not found." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if ! docker compose version &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: 'docker compose' plugin not found." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if [ -d "$NT_INPUT" ]; then
    if ! ls "$NT_INPUT"/*.nt &>/dev/null; then
      echo "ERROR: no .nt files found in $NT_INPUT" >&2
      exit 1
    fi
  elif [ ! -f "$NT_INPUT" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: $NT_INPUT not found." >&2
    echo "  Set NT_INPUT in config.env or copy the file." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
}

cmd_up() {
  check_prereqs
  mkdir -p "$INDEX_DIR" "$LOG_DIR"

  echo "=== Starting QLever + SHMARQL ==="
  echo "  Input:    $NT_INPUT_DIR/$NT_INPUT_GLOB"
  echo "  Index:    $INDEX_DIR"
  compose up -d --wait --wait-timeout 600
  echo ""
  echo "  SHMARQL UI:      http://localhost:$SHMARQL_PORT"
  echo "  SPARQL endpoint: http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo ""
  echo "  MCP: run './setup.sh mcp-add' to register with Claude Code"
}

cmd_down() {
  compose down
}

cmd_status() {
  compose ps
}

cmd_logs() {
  local service="${1:-}"
  case "$service" in
    qlever)
      compose logs -f qlever-goethe-faust \
        | tee "$LOG_DIR/qlever.log"
      ;;
    shmarql)
      compose logs -f shmarql \
        | tee "$LOG_DIR/shmarql.log"
      ;;
    *)
      echo "Usage: ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
      exit 1
      ;;
  esac
}

cmd_mcp_add() {
  echo "Registering MCP server with Claude Code..."
  claude mcp add "$INDEX_NAME" -- \
    docker run --rm -i --network=host \
    ghcr.io/xorwell/mcp-server-qlever:latest \
    -e "http://localhost:$QLEVER_PORT"
  echo "Done. Test with: claude mcp list"
}

case "${1:-}" in
  up)      cmd_up ;;
  down)    cmd_down ;;
  status)  cmd_status ;;
  logs)    cmd_logs "${2:-}" ;;
  mcp-add) cmd_mcp_add ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: ./setup.sh <command>"
    echo "  up        Start QLever + SHMARQL (builds index on first run)"
    echo "  down      Stop all services"
    echo "  status    Show container status"
    echo "  logs      ./setup.sh logs <qlever|shmarql>"
    echo "  mcp-add   Register QLever as Claude Code MCP server"
    echo ""
    echo "Config: copy config.env.example to config.env and edit."
    echo "Active settings:"
    echo "  NT_INPUT=$NT_INPUT"
    echo "  INDEX_DIR=$INDEX_DIR"
    echo "  LOG_DIR=$LOG_DIR"
    echo "  QLEVER_PORT=$QLEVER_PORT"
    echo "  SHMARQL_PORT=$SHMARQL_PORT"
    echo "  INDEX_NAME=$INDEX_NAME"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac
